mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above its 2017 baseline indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the economy is in an expansionary regime.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 100 yield negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 295 signal entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 295 are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 1.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 1.0% is followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations during early-cycle recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts, where manufacturing expands while housing contracts, signals structural economic imbalances that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence metric fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic magnitude below 1.5 in out-of-sample validation.
